# Mainline

Mainline is Git-native memory for coding agents. It gives AI coding agents the historical why before they edit code: abandoned approaches, superseded decisions, reviewer constraints, risks, anti-patterns, and in-flight work.

Canonical site: https://mainline.sh/
Repository: https://github.com/mainline-org/mainline

## Core pages

- Home: https://mainline.sh/
- Docs: https://mainline.sh/docs/
- Spec: https://mainline.sh/spec/
- Install: https://mainline.sh/install/
- Blog essay: https://mainline.sh/blog/why-coding-agents-need-repo-memory/

## Category pages

- Repo memory for coding agents: https://mainline.sh/use-cases/repo-memory-for-coding-agents/
- Engineering intent memory: https://mainline.sh/use-cases/engineering-intent-memory/
- Intent-aware AI PR review: https://mainline.sh/use-cases/ai-pr-review/

## Comparisons

- Mainline vs RAG: https://mainline.sh/compare/rag/
- Mainline vs session memory: https://mainline.sh/compare/session-memory/
- Mainline vs PR descriptions: https://mainline.sh/compare/pr-descriptions/

## Install commands

```bash
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mainline-org/mainline/main/install.sh | bash
mainline doctor --setup
mainline init --actor-name "alice"
```

Alternative:

```bash
go install github.com/mainline-org/mainline@latest
```

## Product boundaries

Mainline is not a Git replacement, PR system, session recorder, productivity dashboard, or RAG index. It records durable engineering intent owned by the repo and exposes that memory before future edits.
